---
title: "Cross-Species Sequence Identity Analysis"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.width = 10,
  fig.height = 7
)
```

## 1. Load packages

```{r libraries}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(cowplot)
library(stringr)
library(ggtree)
library(ape)
library(ggnewscale)

theme_set(
  theme_classic() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "top",
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    )
)
```

## 2. Load the identity matrix

```{r load-data}
id = read.csv("identity_matrix.csv")
# Columns 2:35 are per-species percent-identity values; force them to numeric
id[, 2:35] = lapply(id[, 2:35], as.numeric)
```

## 3. Parse fasta IDs into gene, annotation category, and species count

```{r parse-fasta-ids}
annotation_levels = c("Exon", "Intron", "Distal_Intergenic",
                        "Downstream", "5_UTR", "3_UTR", "Promoter")

annot_pattern = paste(annotation_levels, collapse = "|")

# The trailing "(?:_[0-9A-Za-z]+)?" is a non-capturing group: it matches and
# discards the duplicate suffix without ever storing it.
parse_pattern = paste0(
  "^(.*)_(", annot_pattern, ")_([0-9]+)(?:_[0-9A-Za-z]+)*$"
)

parsed = str_match(id$fasta, parse_pattern)
# parsed columns: [,1] full match, [,2] gene, [,3] annotation, [,4] number

id = id %>%
  mutate(
    gene          = parsed[, 2],
    annotation    = factor(parsed[, 3], levels = annotation_levels),
    n_species = as.integer(parsed[, 4])
  )

# Sanity check: how many fasta IDs failed to parse?
n_unparsed = sum(is.na(id$gene) & id$fasta != "MEAN")
message("Unparsed fasta IDs: ", n_unparsed)
if (n_unparsed > 0) {
  print(id$fasta[is.na(id$gene) & id$fasta != "MEAN"])
}

table(id$annotation, useNA = "ifany")

# Quick check: does percent identity scale with the number of species covered?
summary(lm(MEAN~n_species, id))
```

## 4. Identity by annotation category

```{r annotation-boxplot}
box_by_annot = ggplot(
  filter(id, !is.na(annotation)),
  aes(x = reorder(annotation, MEAN, FUN = median), y = MEAN, fill = annotation)
) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Sequence identity by genomic annotation category",
    x = "Annotation", y = "Mean % identity across species"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

box_by_annot
```

## 5. Per-gene identity ranking

```{r gene-ranking}
gene_ranking = id %>%
  filter(!is.na(gene)) %>%
  group_by(gene) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_identity = mean(MEAN, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_windows = n(),
    pct_exon = 100 * mean(annotation == "Exon", na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_identity))

head(gene_ranking, 20)
tail(gene_ranking, 20)

# Gene(s) covered by the largest number of windows
max = max(gene_ranking$n_windows)
gene_ranking[gene_ranking$n_windows == max,]$gene
```

## 6. Per-species mean identity and tree setup

```{r descriptive}
# The "MEAN" row holds the per-species average identity across all windows
mean_by_sp = id[id$fasta == "MEAN",2:35]

sorted_mean_by_sp = sort(unlist(mean_by_sp), decreasing = TRUE, na.last = TRUE)
sorted_mean_by_sp

res = data.frame(
  species = names(sorted_mean_by_sp),
  mean_identity = sorted_mean_by_sp
)

# Drop the "MEAN" row itself, keep only per-species values
res = res %>%
  filter(species != "MEAN")

# Returns tip labels ordered by their vertical (y) position in the plotted
# tree, so downstream data (e.g. bar/segment plots) can be aligned to it.
get_tip_order = function(tree) {
  d = ggtree::fortify(tree)
  d = d[d$isTip, ]
  d = d[order(d$y), ]
  d$label
}

# Loading the tree and get the order
tree_obj = ape::read.tree("rodents_noamericans.nh")

tip_order = get_tip_order(tree_obj)

# Phenotype table: species name + phenotype category, used to color tree tips
pheno = read.csv("HelderPhenotypes2.csv", header = FALSE, col.names = c("species", "phenotype"))

pheno$phenotype = factor(pheno$phenotype, levels = c("grey", "green", "orange"))

helder_colors = c("grey", "forestgreen", "orange")
helder_labels = c(grey = "Brachydonte", green = "Plan murin", orange = "Hypsodonte")
```

## 7. Tree with per-species identity bar plot

```{r tree-identity-facet, fig.width = 10}
bar_data = res %>%
  rename(label = species) %>%
  select(label, mean_identity)

# Base tree, colored by phenotype
tree_plot_facet = ggtree(tree_obj, layout = "roundrect", size = 0.5) %<+% pheno +
  aes(color = phenotype) +
  geom_tiplab(size = 4, align = TRUE, linetype = "dotted") + 
  geom_tippoint(size = 1.5) +
  geom_treescale(x = 0.5, y = -1, width = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = helder_colors,
    name = "Phénotype",
    labels = helder_labels,
    na.value = "grey60"
  ) +
  theme_tree2() +
  theme(legend.position = "left", plot.margin = margin(1, 2, 10, 10)) +
  new_scale_color()

# Add a facet panel showing each species' mean % identity to mouse as a
# segment, with Mesocricetus_auratus highlighted separately
fig_ab = facet_plot(
  tree_plot_facet,
  panel = "% d'identité moyen de la séquence\n par rapport à la séquence de la souris",
  data = bar_data,
  geom = geom_segment,
  aes(x = 0, xend = mean_identity, y = y, yend = y,
      colour = label == "Mesocricetus_auratus"),
  linewidth = 2
) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "gold", "FALSE" = "red4"), guide = "none")

fig_ab

tree_plot_facet
```
